name: Devil's Food Cake with Fudge Frosting 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Grease two 8-inch layer cake pans well and dust them generously with cocoa powder.
2. Sift the flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt onto a piece of waxed paper and set aside.
3. Cream the butter until fluffy, then add the sugar and vanilla extract. Continue beating until the mixture is light and silvery in color.
4. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the sifted dry ingredients alternately with the boiling water, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Be sure to beat well after each addition.
5. Divide the batter equally between the two prepared pans and smooth the tops.
6. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, or until the layers feel spongy to the touch and begin to pull away from the sides of the pans. Cool the layers upright on wire racks for 10 minutes, then carefully loosen them around the edges with a spatula and transfer onto wire racks. Allow to cool completely.
7. For the frosting, cream the butter until light, then gradually add the confectioners' sugar while beating continuously. Beat in the melted chocolate and vanilla extract, then stir in just enough half-and-half, milk, or cream to achieve a good consistency for spreading.
8. Assemble the cake by sandwiching the two cooled layers with frosting, then frost the top and sides of the cake. Swirl the frosting into peaks and valleys. This recipe yields about 8 servings.
